title: asperula cynanchica var. rupicola
intro: Asperula cynanchica var. rupicola, also known as Alpine Woodruff, is a subspecies of flowering plant belonging to the gentian family. This plant is primarily used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Alpine Woodruff include its ability to relieve digestive issues, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory problems. Alpine Woodruff can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and its leaves and roots are often used in herbal remedies to promote overall well-being.
